content.text: ## Error Type Console Error ## Error Message A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used: - A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`. - Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called. - Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server. - External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML. - Invalid HTML tag nesting. It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded. https://react.dev/link/hydration-mismatch ... <__next_root_layout_boundary__>

I can see the issue. The hydration mismatch is caused by the **Dark Reader browser extension** injecting inline styles (`--darkreader-inline-stroke`, `data-darkreader-inline-stroke`) into SVG elements before React hydrates the page. The server renders without these attributes, but Dark Reader modifies the DOM on the client side.

The fix is to add `suppressHydrationWarning` to your `<html>` element, which is the recommended approach for handling browser extension conflicts:
Now let me also add `suppressHydrationWarning` to the ThemeSwitch button since that's where the error originates:

edits.newText:
<button
      onClick={toggleTheme}
      suppressHydrationWarning
      className={`relative flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-[var(--text-color-primary)] hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity overflow-hidden ${className}`}
    >
edits.oldText:
<button
      onClick={toggleTheme}
      className={`relative flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-[var(--text-color-primary)] hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity overflow-hidden ${className}`}
    >

content.text:
The fix is complete. Here's what was done:

**Changes made:**

1. **`app/layout.tsx`**: Added `suppressHydrationWarning` to the `<html>` element
2. **`components/ui/theme-switch-button.tsx`**: Added `suppressHydrationWarning` to the button element

**What this fixes:**

The hydration mismatch was caused by the **Dark Reader browser extension** injecting these attributes into SVG elements before React hydrates:
- `style={{--darkreader-inline-stroke:"currentColor"}}`
- `data-darkreader-inline-stroke=""`

The server renders SVGs without these attributes, but Dark Reader modifies the DOM on the client side before React can hydrate, causing the mismatch.

`suppressHydrationWarning` tells React to not warn about differences in attributes that are expected to differ between server and client (like browser extension modifications). This is the [recommended solution](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/client/hydrateRoot#suppressing-unavoidable-hydration-mismatch-attributes) from React's official documentation for this exact scenario.

The warning is cosmetic and doesn't affect functionality - the theme switch and everything else will continue to work correctly.
